rationale: The query/hyp engine is a generating-function, locally-tree-approximation analysis of configuration-model-style ensembles (arbitrary degree/incidence distributions) yielding exact phase-transition thresholds, component/core sizes, and distance statistics; the candidate instead studies convergence of extreme eigenvalues of network connectivity matrices via random-matrix/spectral-ensemble reasoning, with no generating functions, tree recursion, or threshold derivation, so the machinery does not match. The candidate's object is also random (scale-free) networks studied via synchronization/epidemic-spreading dynamics — the same network-science substrate as the query's random graphs, not a transfer into the hypothesized random-CSP/theoretical-CS domain, so no genuine cross-field object shift has occurred.
